squire.description:
The most basic of all jobs, and one of two jobs a unit can initially select when created. While its stats are below average in almost every respect, it has a surprising amount of utility in its abilities, especially with Dash and Accumulate. It also has a base Move of 4, allowing it to traverse the map quickly.

Staying as a Squire is generally not recommended, but it can still be quite powerful with the right skills and equipment.
pros: High Move
Accumulate for both JP/EXP gain and strong damage scaling
Dash never misses
Move +1 is a cheap and excellent early-game mobility skill (200 JP)
cons: Low HP pool and growth
Lower PA/MA modifiers than most jobs
Limited utility compared to later jobs
Access to axes, and axes are terrible
No offhand capabilities without external support

chemist.description:
One of two jobs a unit can initially select when created, and secretly one of the stronger late-game powerhouses if developed properly. Early game, it offers incredible utility but struggles with survivability without Auto-Potion. In the late game, high-Brave Chemists can become extremely difficult to kill if you can afford a steady supply of X-Potions. Development as a Chemist is also required to obtain some of the best items in the game via Move-Find Item. It also has access to guns.

A Chemist will often branch into other jobs for development, but it can absolutely remain in a late-game squad without regret.
pros: Access to guns
Auto-Potion is one of the best survivability tools in the game
Item throwing is extremely versatile, from healing to revival
Excellent for early and mid-game JP development
cons: Weak offensive output before acquiring guns
Very low HP multiplier and growth
Item usage can be expensive in the early and mid-game

knight.description:
Physical powerhouses capable of branching into magical utility thanks to their ability to equip robes. Knights function as classic tanks, boasting high HP, strong physical attack, and excellent defensive options with shields. They can break equipment and reduce enemy stats with their abilities, though in practice, stat reduction is often more useful if you plan on stealing gear.

They suffer from poor mobility and struggle against magical threats early on. However, with access to Knight Swords and development in magic-oriented jobs, they can become flexible hybrid units, even if other jobs may ultimately offer stronger endgame utility.
pros: High physical damage, especially in the mid-game
Strong durability at all stages of the game
Access to robes enables hybrid builds
Knight Swords are extremely powerful in the late game
cons: Low mobility
Parry is a weak reaction ability
Knight Swords are unavailable for much of the game
Other jobs can surpass their offensive and defensive utility

archer.description: Exceptionally talented at dealing damage from far away using bows. While its stats leave something to be desired, an Archer positioned on high ground can often take uncontested shots at enemies across the map. Access to Speed Save and Concentrate gives it valuable tools that remain useful across many jobs, though its effectiveness is heavily dependent on map layout. Some maps make Archer feel excellent, while others make it an exceptionally rough experience.
pros: Only job with access to bows
Can deal significant damage from high vantage points
Speed Save and Concentrate are useful on many jobs
Can trivialize much of the early game with good positioning
cons: Lower HP pool than many classes
Low mobility despite relying heavily on height advantage
Charge is one of the weakest skillsets in the game, even with FFTC buffs
Extremely reliant on favorable map positioning
Guns outclass bows in literally every single way possible

whiteMage.description:
The traditional healer of the Final Fantasy series. Excelling at protection, the White Mage is the cornerstone of many early-to-mid game playthroughs in terms of versatility. Holy is an expensive but incredibly powerful assassination spell for boss fights and certain strategies. They also have a higher speed multiplier than most jobs, allowing them to become deceptively fast early in the game.

Because support magic is not guaranteed to hit allies, maintaining high Faith and good compatibility is important for consistency. Their durability is fairly low, but they remain a safe and reliable option for most of the game, even if they lack the offensive punch of Black Mages.
pros: Holy is one of the best assassination tools in the game
High speed multiplier
Excellent suite of support spells for sustaining the party
Consistently strong throughout the game
cons: No access to rods limits offensive magic scaling
Reliant on high Faith and compatibility for consistent support
Low durability

blackMage.description:
The iconic magician of the Final Fantasy series. Excelling at large elemental AoE damage, the Black Mage is a cornerstone of many offensive strategies throughout a playthrough, despite how early it becomes available. With its high MA and access to powerful magic support tools, it can provide strong utility or specialize in overwhelming enemies with devastating spells.

Careless casting can lead to MP shortages or accidental damage to allies. As enemies become faster later in the game, safely casting spells becomes more difficult, requiring careful positioning and timing.
pros: Elemental damage makes many encounters trivial
Highest MA multiplier
Flexible magical utility with proper development
Excellent for farming and grinding strategies
cons: Early-game MP management can be difficult
Extremely low durability
Poor targeting can damage allies
Late-game enemy speed makes casting harder

monk.description:
One of the strongest physical powerhouses in the game, the Monk relies on unarmed combat to deliver massive damage. It also provides access to Counter and First Strike, two excellent reaction skills that make melee encounters far more manageable. With strong self-healing options and a reliance on high Brave, Monks deal more damage than expected while frequently triggering reaction abilities.

Overall, the Monk is an exceptionally well-rounded job with high survivability and strong offensive presence, though it requires some setup to reach its full potential.
pros: Physical damage scales strongly with Brave
Excellent self-sustain through healing abilities
Can attack from range with certain skills
Access to powerful reaction abilities like Counter and First Strike
4 Jump improves terrain traversal compared to many jobs
cons: Can be difficult to develop in the early game
Highly vulnerable to Brave reduction effects
Limited equipment options

thief.description:
A light-fingered rogue who steals equipment as well as hearts. While weak physically, their high mobility allows them to weave in and out of dangerous situations. Steal Heart is deceptively one of the best tools in the early and mid game for survivability, though it can only be used on targets of the opposite sex. As such, having both a male and female user can make certain encounters much easier.

Thieves also enable poaching, which provides access to some of the best equipment in the game, making them highly valuable for long-term progression.
pros: High speed multiplier and excellent speed growth
4 Move natively, with Move +2 allowing exceptional mobility
Stealing equipment enables early access to powerful gear and unique items, such as the Genji set
Steal Heart provides strong crowd control
Poaching is extremely valuable in the late game
cons: Steal Heart is gender-restricted
Expensive to develop
Very low HP pool
Limited to knives, which are generally weak

mystic.description:
Commonly considered one of the weaker caster jobs at first glance, the Mystic specializes in debuffs and HP/MP manipulation. While it has the lowest MA among magic jobs, its late-game utility should not be underestimated. When built correctly, a Mystic can become a powerful enabler, draining resources, crippling enemies, and supporting other casters.

While not always ideal for strict min-maxing due to its stats, Mystic abilities are essential for certain strategies, including setups that trivialize encounters or provide near-infinite MP for allied mages.
pros: Trepidation can drastically reduce enemy Brave
Invigoration deals percentage-based HP damage, making it strong against bosses
Wide weapon selection compared to other casters
Defense Boost is a strong survivability reaction for many jobs
Disbelief enables powerful low-Faith strategies
Near-infinite MP through Empowerment and Manafont
Unique and visually distinct spell animations
cons: Lowest MA among caster jobs
Low spell hit rates early on
Highly reliant on compatibility, which can be inconsistent
Key abilities are expensive to unlock

summoner.description:
Another iconic spellcaster, the Summoner is unique in that its AoE summons only ever benefit allies or harm enemies, even when both are within range. With the second-highest MA in the game behind Black Mage, Summoners are highly effective at delivering powerful magical utility.

However, their low Speed multiplier makes them slower than much of your roster and more vulnerable to fast late-game enemies. It is often recommended to develop Summoner to learn its strongest abilities and then transition to another caster, though Summoners remain powerful units in their own right.
pros: Summons never harm allies or benefit enemies within their AoE
Golem provides exceptional team-wide survivability
Summon magic can be used effectively by other magic jobs
Very high MP pool
High MA enables strong magical output
cons: Low Speed multiplier makes them slower than most jobs
Low survivability
Vulnerable to being eliminated before acting, especially late game

orator.description:
Using their words as weapons, the Orator can disrupt enemies in a variety of ways, from recruiting them to your side to significantly reducing their effectiveness. Their unique talk-based skillset enables strategies that no other job can replicate, especially when it comes to acquiring new units or controlling enemy behavior.

They also have access to guns, giving them solid ranged damage potential. However, outside of their unique abilities, their stats are generally underwhelming, and their overall utility can fall behind other jobs if those abilities are not being actively leveraged.
pros: Can recruit enemies, allowing them to permanently join your team
Recruitment enables early access to powerful equipment from enemies
Access to guns provides reliable ranged damage
Can interact with monsters without requiring additional abilities
Mimic Daravon and Threaten offer excellent utility for many builds
cons: Low stats across the board
Generally outclassed by Chemist for gun-based roles
Talk skills scale with MA, making other jobs better for recruitment in some cases
Highly dependent on compatibility for consistent results

timeMage.description: The King/Queen of support jobs. While their offensive capabilities are severely limited until they learn Meteor, the offensive utility Time Mage presents is absolutely critical to the success of more linear gameplay strategies. They are also implicitly the tankiest mage unit due to their unique ability to take damage in the form of MP before HP, which has no spillover. This means you can put Manafont on them, and they will need more than one person attacking them to ever really die. Also, Quick is probably the most broken spell in the game. They also have Teleport... which is the most broken movement skill in the game!
pros: Access to some of the best buffs and debuffs in the game
High durability with the right skill setup
Often trivializes encounters as soon as mid-game begins
Teleport
Probably the most fun build options for a mage
Honestly, Teleport is just so broken. I had to say it twice for effect here.
cons: Lower than average MA for a mage
You will run into MP issues if you make them tanky frontliners
Meteor is extremely expensive both in JP and MP. You will likely not even be able to cast it until later in the game, and even then not more than once or twice a map

dragoon.description:
Ever wondered what it would be like if Knights sacrificed Knight Swords for infinite jump height, 2-range melee attacks, and Reraise as a reaction skill? Dragoon is that answer. Essentially superior to Knight in many key areas, they boast high PA growth and multipliers, strong weapon options, and extremely consistent vertical mobility. They can even equip robes, allowing for hybrid builds similar to Knights.

However, Jump can be difficult to use effectively and often isn't worth it if you're already in range of the enemy. Despite that, Dragoons are durable, powerful, and excel at assassination when built correctly.
pros: Jump is strong for assassination tactics
Ignore Height is an extremely valuable movement skill
Lances have 2-range, making them excellent for safe melee attacks
High durability and strong PA multiplier at all stages of the game
Dragon Spirit provides Reraise as a reaction ability
cons: No access to lances until Chapter 2
Limited horizontal mobility
Jump can feel unintuitive and difficult to use effectively

geomancer.description:
You either love them or you hate them. Geomancers boast high Move, solid base stats, and strong equipment options. They also have access to Geomancy, a set of map-dependent, free-cast abilities that deal damage and inflict status effects. These abilities scale with MA, which Geomancer has at a fairly average level.

They excel at poking and debuffing enemies from a distance while still functioning as capable backup fighters. However, they can struggle on the frontline without specific setups, such as Ice Brand + Ice Shield healing. Despite this, Geomancers are versatile and bring a surprising amount of utility to any job that leverages their toolkit.
pros: Long-range poking and debuffing through Geomancy
Shields significantly improve survivability
4 Move provides strong positional control
Solid all-around physical stats allow flexible roles
cons: Cannot function as true frontliners due to lack of heavy armor
Geomancy damage output is relatively low
Terrain dependency can limit effectiveness on certain maps
Requires full development to reach peak effectiveness
Axes suck

samurai.description:
One of the more iconic jobs in the game, Samurai utilize a stock of Katanas to unleash a variety of effects on both allies and enemies. They also gain access to Shirahadori, widely considered one of the best reaction abilities in the game, which reduces the hit rate of incoming physical attacks by a large percentage of the unit's Brave.

Samurai are strong frontline fighters with unique tools and strategies, but their full effectiveness comes at a steep cost. Maintaining a supply of Katanas for Iaido can be expensive, making them less appealing during the mid game when they first become available. If you want to properly support a Samurai, be prepared to invest heavily in resources. That said, they are powerful, versatile, and honestly just look really cool.
pros: High PA combined with Shirahadori makes for exceptional survivability against physical attacks
Two Hands greatly boosts damage output with many weapon types
Katana abilities provide a wide range of utility and effects
Strong offensive presence with flexible build options
cons: Low mobility and below-average HP make them vulnerable to magic
Katanas can break when used for Iaido abilities
Very expensive to maintain
Requires grinding to sustain resources
No access to Katanas until midway through Chapter 2

ninja.description:
Many consider Ninja to be the best job in the game, and for good reason. They have the highest Speed growth and multiplier, allowing them to act quickly—often multiple times before enemies, or even your own units, can respond when built effectively. They can naturally dual wield, enabling two attacks per action, which more than compensates for their lower PA. Throwing is also an excellent utility skill.

With incredible mobility, utility, and damage output, Ninja dominate most encounters. Their only real weakness is extremely low durability, with one of the lowest HP multipliers and growths in the game. Does that matter when everything dies first? Usually not. Just… don’t let them get charmed.
pros: Best-in-class offensive capability
Highest Speed growth and multiplier
Innate dual wield enables two attacks per action
Excellent mobility with strong Move and Jump
Throwing provides cheap and effective ranged options
Can trivialize much of the game once developed
cons: Extremely low durability
Highly vulnerable to magic damage
Positioning mistakes can lead to instant death

arithmetician.description:
Simultaneously the worst and best job in the game. Arithmeticians are slower than molasses, have terrible base stats, and effectively cannot function in combat through normal means. So what gives?

The answer is mathematics. Arithmeticians can cast any non-summon spell they have learned from other magical schools instantly, for free, and without charge time, based on specific criteria such as level, CT, EXP, JP, or map position.

This enables some of the most infamous strategies in the game, such as using Holy with Math Skill to wipe entire armies with zero risk when paired with equipment like Chameleon Robe. While staying in the job itself is almost never worthwhile, transferring Math Skill to another job removes every downside.

Be aware: relying on this will absolutely trivialize your game. You’ve been warned.
pros: Math
cons: Extremely low stats, multipliers, and growths
Incredibly slow Speed multiplier
Practically unusable in direct combat
Must be paired with other jobs to be effective
Seriously, their speed is unbelievably bad

bard.description:
The weaker of the two gender-locked jobs, Bards are only available to male units and rely on MA for their healing effectiveness. Their songs apply map-wide effects that only benefit allies, which sounds powerful on paper. However, outside of healing songs, the actual stat boosts from Bard abilities are randomly applied, making them inconsistent.

Like several other niche jobs, staying in Bard is generally not recommended due to poor HP and PA growths. That said, their abilities can still be useful in niche strategies or challenge runs. They are also the only job with access to harps, though only a few are actually worth using.
pros: Can repeatedly and infinitely increase ally stats during a battle
Harps provide a serviceable ranged damage option
Move +3 is an extremely powerful mobility skill
cons: Poor growths and multipliers
Requires progressing through multiple magic jobs to unlock
Male-only, while female units are generally stronger for MA-based roles
Randomized song effects reduce consistency
Generally less effective than Dancer

dancer.description:
The stronger of the two gender-locked jobs, Dancer focuses on damaging and debuffing enemies rather than supporting allies like Bard. While their growths are generally poor, they make up for it with the ability to trivialize encounters through powerful map-wide debuffs.

With abilities like Slow Dance reducing enemy Speed to a crawl, or turning entire battlefields into frogs (yes), Dancers can completely dismantle enemy teams over time. While it is usually not ideal to remain in the job long-term, the utility they provide can turn difficult fights into effortless victories.
pros: Slow Dance can trivialize many encounters
Can win fights without needing to reposition
Strong map-wide debuff utility
4 Dancers and 1 Bard is the most fun you can have online
cons: Poor weapon selection
Dances are RNG-based, similar to Bard songs
Extremely poor growths and base stats

mime.description:
Incredibly difficult to unlock, Mime is a unique job that has no abilities of its own and instead mimics actions previously taken by other units on the map. This can be either extremely powerful or completely useless depending on the situation.

What truly stands out are its base stats and growths, which are among the highest in the game for PA, MA, and HP. Mime is central to deleveling strategies that can push units far beyond normal limits. While it can offer immense utility through mimicking powerful abilities like dances, math skills, and summons, it is also one of the hardest jobs to use effectively.

Recommended for advanced players… or anyone who just wants to see what kind of chaos happens.
pros: Best growths in the game for deleveling strategies
Extremely high durability
Can mimic powerful abilities instantly at no cost
Base Move/Jump of 4 allows strong positioning
cons: No access to weapons or armor. Good luck!
Extremely difficult to use effectively. Good luck!
Highly dependent on team composition. Good luck!
